description: Apollo 9 Flight Journal: Day 1: Orbit 4 - SPS-1

tokens: We show ignition on the S-IVB.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: It's just like a bright star disappearing into the distance.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Is there quite a bit of debris kicked out there, Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Looked like a real clean burn.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: You could see a lot of stuff coming out when he just started up, but then it just went into a nice bright light.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We got some movies, but I'm not sure they're going to be too good.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: And the S-IVB has shut down, Apollo 9?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: He's just a speck in the distance right now.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Now that we've got him out of the way, back with the business at hand.
I'm ready to read SPS-1 PAD any time.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Could we have P00 and Accept?
We'd like to start you up a target load.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Starting with the P30, and there will be about a minute delay on the target load.
Starting now on the maneuver PAD.
SPS-1, 005:59 all zips, plus 0036.8 all zips, all zips, 0036.8 0032.4 0:05.1 58840, plus 1.00 minus 0.20 17 13.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You cut out very badly in that.
I got T and I got ΔV and ΔV and ΔV and that's all I got.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'll try you again, Now are you reading me, okay?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, I won't read the T again.
That's 5:59:00, and reading the ΔV, plus 0036.8, and are you with me?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Yes I am, and that's as far as we got last time.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The last thing I got was 58840; you got any more?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You're coming in a little weak.
Understand you got up through CSM weight; is that affirmative?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Readback: 005:59 all zips, plus 0036.8 all zips all zips 0036.8 0032.4 0:05.1 58840, plus 1.00, minus 0.20 17 135.20 33.100.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I would also now like to give you your gimbal angles used in the PAD REFSMMAT for SPS-1.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And this is affirmative; Houston confirms.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Did you get the P27 in?
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Ascension.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We'd like to update your state vector again, if you could give us P00 and Accept.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And a couple of items: There is a bias in your X-[axis] PIPA, and we are taking a look at this.
The only steps we would like to do at this time would be to recommend that you stay in average G as little time as possible.

We are estimating that during the SPS-1 burn, there will be an error of about a foot and a half, and so the only thing we will do at this time is you just come out of average G as soon as possible, and we will talk about this later after the burn.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And we have another problem here.
Our O flow high light continues to come on and we seem to have a steady-state flow around 7-8/10 pounds per hour.
We don't have the vent open yet - the waste management vent, but we do have the LM pressurization on, and I'm wondering if you could give us a clue as to whether you think we have a leaky LM or what.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And stand by for some words of wisdom on that.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're getting the Master Alarm light on every few minutes here, either from the hydrogen-cryo or the O flow high.
It is almost like the simulator.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We'd like to have the fans in H tank 1 turned on manually at this time, and just leave it on.
We will leave it on for a while and take a look at it.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Okay, And we would like to know if you can see the docking angle index when you were up in the tunnel.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: The computer is yours and I have a state vector - I mean a nav check to go along with that state vector.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Ready to copy your nav check.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We have checked it here on the ground.
Unless you want it, I won't read it to you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We are going to have you for about another minute at Ascension and then we will see you over Tananarive at 19.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We would like to have you turn off the LM pressurization valve to see if that takes care of the O high flow.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We will catch that in a few minutes and advise you.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: We want you to go back to P30 - P40 again to recompute that REFSMMAT after this uplink.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Hello, Apollo 9, this is Houston through Tananarive.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: If you read me, we'll see you over Carnarvon at around 32.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're reading you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: (garbled) [torquing] angles if you are ready to copy.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Just a word of info to close out that item on the power going into the LM.
That duty cycle has now settled down and is exactly the same as the duty cycle was prior to launch.
So everything is good im the LM power.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Guam.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We're rushing through a checklist here.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Apollo 9, Houston, I will have you here for about another 2½ minutes, and I have got a couple of words of wisdom on your attitude on this burn - why you are going to be off a couple of degrees in pitch and a couple of degrees in yaw if you want me to give them to you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Your REFSMMAT is off slightly and we think this may have come about by the order in which you loaded the DAP in relation to the P52.
However, we have taken a look at this and we are saying at burn attitude you are going to have zero roll, a pitch of 358, and a yaw of about 002, and this will give you the right burn.
It's just you won't be at 000 on the ball.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We seem to have our O thing in hand now.
We have closed the tunnel thing and we also (garbled) suit (garbled) valve, and one of us had our helmet off for just a moment there and that was contributing to it, too.
So it looks like we have the O problem in hand.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Some of it was dropped out.
I am about to lose you here.
We will see you over Hawaii at 57.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: A minute 10 seconds, ready to go.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Delta-V to Auto; EMS to Auto.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: The LM is still there, by God!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: -- to get the Delta-V thrust, off.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: Gimbal motors, four, to off, one at a time.
transcript.speaker: Schweickart

tokens: I copy your residuals: plus 1.8, plus 0.5, minus 0.2.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: In about 30 seconds we'll lose you off Hawaii and have you back at Redstone about a minute later.
There will be a break in there, and then we'll pick you up for a long pass.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Apollo 9, this is Houston through Redstone.
We ought to have you now on a long stateside pass.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: You're down a little bit, Dave, but I'm reading you okay.
We've got you through the Redstone now, and it should be a nice long pass.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: I think we'll get better here in a couple of minutes.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Trying again; how do you read?
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Houston, Apollo 9, We read you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're reading you five-square, We just sent the S-IVB hyperbolic and got it out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We were reading you all along there.
I guess you just weren't reading us.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: Guess we had some of our receivers turned in on the S-IVB there, that I didn't know they had taken away from me.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: When you get squared away after the burn, I've got your star count update for you.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Star count update: 006:49:45.00 068 - 068.0 if you want the decimal there - 291.1 330.2.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Houston confirms the update and would like to have you go ahead and open up the LM pressurization valve, if you concur.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: We tried to get a hold of you before to tell you we're going to do it, so we'll open it up at this time.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: How are we making out on RCS as opposed to manual?
What I'm wondering about is whether or not we should do the star count.
transcript.speaker: McDivitt

tokens: We're down a little bit, but we're got an excellent margin, and nobody is sweating it at all, Jim.
We recommend that you go ahead and do this star check.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: Let me give you an up[date] on the SPS PU system there.
Following the burn, I'm reading 89.2 percent in oxidizer and 93.7 in fuel and an unbalanced pegged on the decreased side.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Copy 89.2, 93.7, and the unbalanced pegged on the decreased side.
transcript.speaker: Roosa

tokens: And for your information, the feed vent, SPS injector valve A-1 opens slower than A-2.
transcript.speaker: Scott
